title: DNS resolution not working as expected with configured search domain in host config
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

When you have set a search domain in your hosts ```/etc/resolv.conf``` then the name resolution in podman container is failing, until you add the ```dns.podman``` search domain also to the ```/etc/resolv.conf``` of your host.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. add a search entry on your host to the ```/etc/resolv.conf``` file (example: search sample.domain)

2. start 2 container by using docker-compose without specifing explicit a network (dnsname plugin is installed)

3. from container01 try:
```
bash$ nslookup container02
```

4. from container01 try:
```
bash$ nslookup container02.dns.podman
```


**Describe the results you received:**
result from step 3:
```
Server:         10.89.0.1
Address:        10.89.0.1:53

** server can't find containername.sample.domain: NXDOMAIN
```

result from step 4:
```
Server:         10.89.0.1
Address:        10.89.0.1:53

Name:   container02.dns.podman
Address: 10.89.0.118
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
i want to use just the container-name for communication between containers. The search domain ```dns.podman``` should automatically added as first search domain in the ```/etc/resolv.conf``` of the container.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
As work arround you can add ```dns.podman``` as search domain to your hosts ```/etc/resolv.conf```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.3
API Version:  3.2.3
Go Version:   go1.15.7
Built:        Thu Jul 29 17:02:43 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

No i haven't tested with the latest version because Version 3.2.3 is the highest available in the RedHat Package Repo.
Yes i searched in the Troubleshooting guide, and no the is no hint for this issue. 

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Virtual Maschine Running on VMware

comments.body: Can you show the content in `/etc/resolv.conf` on the host and in the container?

This works for me and I have a search domain in `/etc/resolv.conf`
```
version: "3"
services:
  con1:
    image: alpine
    command: cat /etc/resolv.conf
  con2:
    image: alpine
    command: nslookup con1

```

sure, in the host /etc/resolv.conf:
```
# Generated by NetworkManager
search sample.domain
nameserver 10.0.0.2
nameserver 10.0.0.3
```
In the container /etc/resolv.conf:
```
search sample.domain
nameserver 10.89.0.1
```

Can you share your docker-compose file?
In my container the `dns.podman` search domain is set correctly:
```
search dns.podman
nameserver 10.89.0.1
```

could this be related to this issue?
https://github.com/containers/dnsname/issues/17

on rhel-8.4 the maximum dnsmasq version is currently ```2.79-15.el8``` not ```2.80-13.fc31```
can you run `podman network create testnet` and `podman run  --log-level debug  --rm --network testnet alpine cat /etc/resolv.conf` and provide the full log please.
Can you file a bugzilla this seems to be a old dnsname version in RHEL.

yes I can file a bugzilla.

From your output I can confirm that the dnsname version is to old, it should be at least v1.2
Okay thanks for the Help. I had filed the Bug and mentioned this Issue.
